why: Pure stubbornness. No Chaos and no complex mechanics. Simply build a wall of stone and crush everything. Perfect for warming up.
tip: Stick to Tunneller stacks and let the enemy come to you. Armour-piercing artillery is your best friend.
keyMechanic: Grudge Book – every defeat suffered is recorded and must be avenged.

why: The quintessential all-rounder. Cavalry infantry artillery and magic – everything is available. Ideal for getting back into the game.
tip: Unite the Elector Counts early – the political mechanic is simple and rewarding. Volkmar is easier for true beginners.
keyMechanic: Elector Counts – individual provincial lords must be diplomatically united or subjugated.

why: Brain-off aggression. Simply charge forward kill everything and trigger the Waagh! No strategic thinking required.
tip: Stack Black Orcs and ride Grimgor into battle – he is a duel monster. Never lose the Waagh! momentum.
keyMechanic: Waagh! – once sufficient Orc strength is reached a free ghost army is automatically spawned.

why: Fill undead armies through battles with no recruitment costs. Simple economic concept with atmospheric troops.
tip: Raise Dead after every battle saves enormous costs. Mannfred is a caster AND a fighter.
keyMechanic: Undead Regeneration – fallen units are replenished on the battlefield through Necromancy.

why: Ulthuan as an island starting position is relaxed. Troops are expensive but powerful. A good introduction to Elven diplomacy.
tip: No early expansion pressure – gather gold research technologies and then strike.
keyMechanic: Intrigue Pool – diplomatic manoeuvres using a shared resource pool with other High Elf factions.

why: Dinosaurs as an army. Simple mechanics powerful frontline monsters and no complicated resource management.
tip: Carnosaur and Stegadon create an unstoppable frontline. Kroq-Gar himself is a battle-decider.
keyMechanic: Geomantic Web – connect temple-cities for powerful campaign bonuses.

why: Massive Zerg-rush feel with insane technology. The atomic bomb as a weapon. Medium learning curve but enormous fun factor.
tip: Warplightning Cannon destroys everything. Invest early in weapons research. Betray everyone – that is Skaven tradition.
keyMechanic: Undermine – place nests beneath enemy cities for passive influence gains.

why: Corsair playstyle with Black Arks. The teleport mechanic makes it uniquely mobile and aggressive on the campaign map.
tip: Black Ark teleport gives free surprise attacks. Learn this mechanic early and use it for rapid expansion.
keyMechanic: Black Arks – floating fortresses that can be moved across the world's oceans.

why: The dragon transformation playstyle is unique. Cathay has exciting Silk Road trade mechanics and feels fresh compared to WH1 and WH2.
tip: Maintain the balance between Yin and Yang. Miao Ying's dragon transformation is extremely powerful – use it in critical battles.
keyMechanic: Yin/Yang Balance – too much of one side incurs penalties; balance grants powerful bonuses.

why: Complex economic system with slave labour and industry. One of the deepest campaign loops in the entire game.
tip: Slave production is the engine – without slaves nothing works. Prioritise raids and pillaging from the very start.
keyMechanic: Slave Economy – slaves as the central resource for buildings units and technology.

why: Unique ice magic focus and a defensive playstyle with the Ataman vassal diplomacy system. Demanding but deeply satisfying.
tip: Prioritise the Ice Court mechanic – it gives you enormously powerful wizards early in the game. Kostaltyn is the hardest LL in the game.
keyMechanic: Ice Court and Vassal System – grant land to Atamans in exchange for loyalty and bonuses.

why: The grand finale. Juggle all four Chaos Gods and play as a global threat. Archaon can confederate all other WoC lords.
tip: Choose one Chaos God as your main patron – do not try to maximise all of them at once.
keyMechanic: Chaos Gods Patronage – choose a god as your patron for powerful units and campaign bonuses.

why: The freshest Slaanesh experience. The Thrall Camps mechanic feels unique. Rewards an aggressive non-linear playstyle.
tip: Sack settlements quickly and build Thrall Camps – your economy will explode. Do not go defensive too early.
keyMechanic: Thrall Camps – build camps in raided settlements for passive income without administration.

why: The newest Norsca playstyle with a Chaos Altar ritual system and mutation magic. Perfect for Chaos fans who want something fresh.
tip: Build altars as early as possible – the tier upgrades through rituals are powerful. Sayl is an excellent wizard.
keyMechanic: Chaos Altar Rituals – sacrifice resources for permanent mutation upgrades to your army.

why: Pure naval warfare fantasy. No fixed capital – you rule from the sea. Completely different feel from classic High Elf campaigns.
tip: Dragonships are your base – protect them well and use coastal raids for income. Avoid land battles where possible.
keyMechanic: Sea Throne – your floating capital on the sea that you must protect.

why: Pure cavalry fantasy. Line up your knights and smash through everything. One of the most satisfying charge animations in the game and very forgiving economy.
tip: Always lead with Knights of the Realm and keep peasant bowmen behind. Chivalry points unlock your strongest units – fight honourably.
keyMechanic: Chivalry System – performing heroic acts in battle earns Chivalry which unlocks Bretonnian Grail units.

why: Fast aggressive raiding campaign with powerful monsters. No complex economy – just raid loot and conquer. Great second campaign after Greenskins.
tip: Raid every settlement you can early on – income comes from pillaging not from holding territory. Wulfrik's teleport ability is extremely powerful for surprise attacks.
keyMechanic: Monster Hunting – hunt specific monsters across the map to unlock powerful Regiment of Renown units.

why: A horde faction that lives entirely in forests and ambushes enemies. No settlements to manage – just constant aggressive movement. Unique and atmospheric playstyle.
tip: Never stop moving. A stationary Beastmen army bleeds Devotion. Ambush stance is your greatest weapon – use it every single turn you can.
keyMechanic: The Call of the Beastmen – Beastmen generate Devotion through battles which fuels their unique horde growth and army unlocks.

why: Living entirely within Athel Loren the Wood Elves have a completely different resource system based on Amber. Elite troops but a very different strategic approach.
tip: Amber is everything – prioritise Amber income above all else. Do not try to hold territory outside Athel Loren early on. Let the forest defend you.
keyMechanic: Amber Economy – Amber is the primary resource used to recruit the most powerful Wood Elf units and unlock technologies.

why: Undead armies with zero upkeep cost and powerful constructs. The economy works completely differently – Canopic Jars replace gold for the most powerful units.
tip: Prioritise the Mortuary Cult early. Sacrificing Canopic Jars for Tomb Scorpions and Sphinxes wins campaigns. Settra himself is an unstoppable combat lord.
keyMechanic: Mortuary Cult – spend Canopic Jars to craft powerful magic items and summon legendary constructs outside of the normal recruitment system.

why: Undead pirates with a unique Infamy system and powerful naval presence. Combines the no-upkeep economy of Vampire Counts with aggressive coastal raiding.
tip: Coastal raids generate massive Infamy which unlocks the most powerful units. Prioritise ships and coastal settlements over inland expansion.
keyMechanic: Infamy System – raiding and winning battles builds Infamy which is used to recruit legendary Pirate Captains and unlock powerful campaign bonuses.

why: Pure aggression turned into a resource. Every kill in battle generates Blood Tithe which summons increasingly powerful Daemonic units mid-fight. Never stop attacking.
tip: Never end a turn without fighting. Blood Tithe decays if you do not battle constantly. Stack Skarbrand's rage abilities and he becomes literally unkillable in melee.
keyMechanic: Blood Tithe – killing enemies in battle generates Blood Tithe points which can be spent to summon powerful Khornate Daemon units directly onto the battlefield.

why: Slow methodical corruption-based campaign. Spread diseases across the map and let attrition do the work before your armies even arrive. Deeply satisfying in a disgusting way.
tip: Spread corruption into enemy territory before moving your armies. Let plague weaken their stacks first. Nurgle armies are slow but nearly impossible to kill.
keyMechanic: Garden of Nurgle – cultivate unique plagues in Nurgle's Garden and unleash them across the campaign map to weaken and corrupt enemy factions.

why: The most magic-focused faction in the game. Kairos manipulates fate itself with Destiny Dice that can be spent to alter campaign and battle outcomes. High skill ceiling.
tip: Master the Destiny Dice system before anything else – knowing when to spend them is the difference between winning and losing. Kairos in battle is a walking magical apocalypse.
keyMechanic: Destiny Dice – a pool of dice rolled at the start of each campaign turn that can be spent to alter battle results change diplomacy outcomes and trigger unique events.

why: Ogres work as mercenaries for other factions via the Contracts system earning massive gold bonuses. Small elite armies that punch far above their weight class.
tip: Take every Contract you can early – the gold income is enormous and it forces you into aggressive play which suits Ogres perfectly. Mournfang Cavalry wins battles.
keyMechanic: Contracts System – accept contracts from other factions to attack their enemies earning huge gold rewards and unlocking Ogre-specific bonuses.

why: The fastest faction in the game with a unique Temptation mechanic that turns enemy lords into followers. Rewarding but requires deep understanding of the mechanics.
tip: Focus on capturing enemy lords through Temptation rather than killing them. A tempted enemy lord joining you is worth more than a dead one. Speed is your greatest weapon.
keyMechanic: Temptation – defeating enemy lords in battle gives the option to tempt them to Slaanesh's cause turning them into powerful followers with unique abilities.

why: The ultimate Chaos experience. Build your Daemon Prince from scratch choosing mutations from all four Chaos Gods. Every campaign is different depending on your choices.
tip: Plan your mutation path before starting. Mixing too many gods weakens you – commit to one or two for maximum power. The late-game Daemon Prince is one of the strongest lords in the game.
keyMechanic: Daemonic Mutations – collect Souls by completing campaign objectives to purchase physical mutations that permanently enhance your Daemon Prince's stats and abilities.
